what_it_does: A single preview card (HTML) for the ConceptV design system that displays an overview of the icon library: a grid of ~36 selected SVG glyphs rendered from a shared icon data object, plus a lead paragraph stating the library has 99 single-stroke 1.5px 24x24 icons and how to render them in React.
observations: Opens with a @dsCard HTML comment carrying card metadata (group='Brand', name='Icon library — overview', subtitle, viewport='700x320') — a structured annotation consumed by a gallery tool.
Links _card.css for shared card styling and uses CSS custom properties throughout (--font-body, --fg-secondary, --accent-gold, --accent-bronze, --font-mono).
Loads icon data from ../assets/icons/cv-icons.js exposing window.CV_ICONS.data keyed by icon name.
A hardcoded SHOW array lists ~36 icon names; script iterates and injects inline SVGs (viewBox 0 0 24 24, stroke=var(--accent-bronze), stroke-width 1.5).
Skips any name not present in CV_ICONS.data (if (!body) return) rather than erroring.
References a separate explorer page at ../assets/icons/index.html and the React component <CvIcon name='…'/>.
A meta row names icon categories: People, Files, Comms, Architecture, Browser, Actions, System, Logic, Platform.
standouts: States '99 individual icons' as a single source of truth in assets/icons/svg/.
Renders glyphs by reading a shared CV_ICONS.data object rather than embedding SVG markup — consistent with the design system's reference-not-copy principle.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard annotation + _card.css + CSS-variable conventions used across preview cards.
Uses var() tokens for colour/type rather than literals in the styled rules (though some inline styles repeat font/size literals).
concerns: The SHOW list of icon names is hand-maintained and can silently fall out of sync with the actual icon set (missing names are silently skipped).
Inline style attributes in the lead paragraph repeat font-family/size/color literals rather than classes.
notes: A brand/preview card that showcases a curated subset of the 99-icon library by pulling glyphs from the shared cv-icons.js data object.
questions: Is the SHOW subset meant to stay representative as icons are added, or is drift expected?
Where is the count '99' authoritatively derived — is it validated against assets/icons/svg/?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard says 'Icon library — overview / 99 SVG icons, single source of truth' and the file renders an overview grid sourced from the single icon data object.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth (icons defined once, referenced everywhere)
Self-describing preview cards consumed by a gallery
intention_signals: Aims to give a visual at-a-glance proof that the icon library exists, is unified (one stroke style/grid), and is consumable from both React and a data object — a showcase of the icon registry's coherence.
novelty: The card renders live SVGs by reading a shared JS data object at runtime rather than hardcoding markup, demonstrating the reference-not-copy discipline in a preview surface.
contribution: Contributes a brand-level overview preview of the icon library and the convention of rendering icons from the shared CV_ICONS data object within a @dsCard gallery card.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML preview card rendering a curated grid of SVG icons from the ConceptV design system's shared cv-icons.js data object, with a lead describing the 99-icon single-source library.
intention_for_embedding: To visually showcase the unified icon library as a single source of truth, renderable in React and from one icon data object.
